rawText:
Lebanon, a country whose modern history reads as a cautionary tale about the co-existence of state institutions with parallel systems of armed power, now finds itself caught between an Israeli military campaign that has intensified into the heart of Beirut, an Iranian patron whose Revolutionary Guards are fracturing under American and Israeli bombardment, and a fleeting window of opportunity in which the Lebanese state could, if it musters the political will, finally assert the monopoly on force that sovereignty demands.

The case against a deep Israeli incursion into Lebanese territory rests on hard-won experience -- articulated by Yair Golan, a former deputy chief of staff who commanded the northern sector and was wounded there -- that Israel's finest strategic hours were those in which decisive military victories were converted into diplomatic agreements, from the 1948 armistice that gave the young state breathing room to the 1973 separation-of-forces accords that led to the peace with Egypt, whereas the failure to anchor the 1967 victory in a political settlement produced not more security but a war of attrition, relentless terrorism, and an unresolved Palestinian conflict.

Israel's 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon, which most Israelis remember not for its tactical successes but for the blood and mud that consumed a generation of soldiers and their families, demonstrates what happens when military presence becomes an end in itself rather than a lever for negotiated outcomes -- a lesson that the current government appears unwilling to absorb even as it sells the public the dangerous illusion that firepower alone can dismantle Hizbullah and guarantee security for the north.

The paradox at the heart of the Iran war is that, while American and Israeli strikes have weakened the IRGC to a degree few thought possible -- sinking more than 100 ships, destroying missile depots and drone factories, killing over 3,000 Iranians -- the organisation's resilience is sustained by a cellular structure of up to 30,000 independent units designed to operate even if the governmental chain of command collapses, a contingency plan that makes the fantasy of neat regime change through air power alone precisely that: a fantasy.

Yet the IRGC is far more divided than its outward cohesion suggests, for an internal intelligence assessment reportedly found that half of its rank and file view membership as merely a job they would abandon for better employment, while much of the ideologically committed remainder is alienated by the corruption of senior commanders, misguided leadership policies, and the deeply unpopular order to turn weapons against Iran's own citizens during the protest waves that have periodically convulsed the country.

For Lebanon, the practical implications of Iran's fracturing are immense: Hizbullah, which has long depended on the IRGC's Quds Force for arms, training, and financial support channelled through opaque networks such as the Al-Qard al-Hassan lending institution, now faces the prospect that its Iranian lifeline will be severed or diminished -- a vulnerability that, combined with the military devastation Israel has already inflicted, creates what may be the most favourable conditions in a generation for the Lebanese state to reclaim authority over its own territory.

The five-step plan advanced by the Lebanese parliamentarian Fouad Makhzoumi -- declaring Beirut a weapons-free city, deploying the army across every district including those long treated as untouchable, establishing permanent checkpoints, granting the armed forces full authority to seize illegal weapons, and implementing a strategic doctrine for civilian protection -- is conceptually straightforward, yet its implementation would require a degree of political courage that Lebanon's consensus-paralysed elites have consistently failed to muster, preferring the deferral of difficult decisions to the risk of confronting Hizbullah's entrenched dual identity as both political party and armed militia.

The most promising path to a stable post-war order in both Iran and Lebanon may run through the kind of broad-based political transition envisioned by Mohsen Sazegara, one of the IRGC's own founders, who argues that a National Reconciliation Council incorporating republicans, monarchists, ethnic groups, and even supporters of the current system could redirect the loyalty of disillusioned guardsmen away from a discredited theocracy and toward the defence of Iranian territory and its people -- though such an outcome would require America to make the council's formation an explicit condition for ending the war, rather than pursuing the more seductive but ultimately hollow goal of picking Iran's next leader as it did in Venezuela.

What unites the crises in Lebanon and Iran is the stubborn lesson that external military force, however overwhelming, cannot substitute for internal political legitimacy: Israel cannot bomb Hizbullah out of Lebanese politics any more than America can bomb the Islamic Republic into a stable democracy, and the attempt to do so risks deepening the very instability it aims to resolve -- producing not order but competing centres of armed power in Tehran and a Beirut that remains, as it has for decades, a capital governed by ambiguity.

The window for converting military gains into durable political settlements -- in Lebanon through the structured assertion of state sovereignty over Hizbullah's parallel army, and in Iran through a negotiated transition that offers the IRGC's rank and file a future worth choosing over martyrdom -- will not remain open indefinitely, and if it closes because strategists in Jerusalem, Washington, and the Gulf mistook the destruction of buildings for the transformation of societies, the region will be left not with peace but with the certainty of another round of violence, and then another, in the mud and rubble that always outlast the bombs.

overview.title: The Lebanon Question: Strategy, Chaos, and the Limits of Regime Change

sentences.original: Lebanon, a country whose modern history reads as a cautionary tale about the co-existence of state institutions with parallel systems of armed power, now finds itself caught between an Israeli military campaign that has intensified into the heart of Beirut, an Iranian patron whose Revolutionary Guards are fracturing under American and Israeli bombardment, and a fleeting window of opportunity in which the Lebanese state could, if it musters the political will, finally assert the monopoly on force that sovereignty demands.
sentences.translation: 레바논은 국가 기관과 무장 세력의 병존에 관한 경고의 역사를 가진 나라로, 지금 베이루트 한복판까지 격화된 이스라엘 군사 작전과, 미국과 이스라엘의 폭격 아래 균열 중인 혁명수비대를 후원국으로 둔 이란, 그리고 정치적 의지만 모으면 주권이 요구하는 무력 독점을 마침내 주장할 수 있는 짧은 기회의 창 사이에 끼여 있다.

grammarElements.text: Lebanon, a country whose modern history reads as a cautionary tale
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 삽입 동격절

grammarElements.text: now finds itself caught between
grammarElements.label: 핵심 동사구

grammarElements.text: an Israeli military campaign ... an Iranian patron ... and a fleeting window of opportunity
grammarElements.label: 세 항목 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: if it musters the political will
grammarElements.label: 조건 삽입절

vocabulary.example: The company's collapse became a cautionary tale for other startups.
The ceasefire offered only a fleeting window of calm.

expressions.usage: 국가 주권의 핵심 기능인 합법적 폭력 독점을 이야기할 때 쓰는 정치학 표현

patterns.explanation: 어떤 주체가 여러 상충하는 세력 사이에 끼인 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The firm found itself caught between regulators, shareholders, and public opinion.
The country finds itself caught between economic pressure, diplomatic isolation, and internal unrest.

drillQuestions.question: Lebanon now finds itself ______ between an Israeli military campaign, an Iranian patron, and a fleeting window of opportunity.

sentences.notes: 한 문장 안에 레바논이 처한 세 갈래 상황을 병렬로 배치해 복잡한 지정학적 딜레마를 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Lebanon, a country whose modern history reads as a cautionary tale
an Israeli military campaign that has intensified into the heart of Beirut
koreanAlignment.ko: 베이루트 한복판까지 격화된 이스라엘 군사 작전

koreanAlignment.en: an Iranian patron whose Revolutionary Guards are fracturing
a fleeting window of opportunity
the monopoly on force that sovereignty demands

sentences.original: The case against a deep Israeli incursion into Lebanese territory rests on hard-won experience -- articulated by Yair Golan, a former deputy chief of staff who commanded the northern sector and was wounded there -- that Israel's finest strategic hours were those in which decisive military victories were converted into diplomatic agreements, from the 1948 armistice that gave the young state breathing room to the 1973 separation-of-forces accords that led to the peace with Egypt, whereas the failure to anchor the 1967 victory in a political settlement produced not more security but a war of attrition, relentless terrorism, and an unresolved Palestinian conflict.
sentences.translation: 이스라엘의 레바논 영토 깊숙이 진격하는 것에 반대하는 논거는 쉽게 얻지 못한 경험에 기반한다 -- 북부 전역을 지휘하다 부상당한 전직 참모차장 야이르 골란이 분명히 밝혔듯 -- 이스라엘의 최고의 전략적 순간은 결정적 군사 승리를 외교 협정으로 전환한 때였으며, 1948년 신생 국가에 숨통을 틔워준 휴전부터 1973년 이집트와의 평화로 이어진 병력분리 협정까지가 그러했고, 반면 1967년 승리를 정치적 합의에 고정시키는 데 실패한 것은 더 큰 안보가 아니라 소모전과 끈질긴 테러, 그리고 미해결 팔레스타인 분쟁을 낳았다.

grammarElements.text: The case against a deep Israeli incursion
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: rests on
grammarElements.label: 핵심 동사 (의존하다)

grammarElements.text: hard-won experience
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: whereas the failure to anchor the 1967 victory in a political settlement produced not more security but ...
grammarElements.label: 대조 접속사절 (whereas)

vocabulary.example: The incursion met fierce resistance from local militia forces.
The armistice gave both sides time to regroup and negotiate.

expressions.usage: 큰 희생이나 오랜 시행착오를 거쳐 체득한 교훈을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 특정 행동에 반대하는 논거의 기반을 제시하는 패턴
examples: The case against deregulation rests on the financial crisis of 2008.
The case against hasty withdrawal rests on lessons learned in Iraq.

drillQuestions.question: The case against a deep Israeli incursion into Lebanese territory ______ on hard-won experience.

sentences.notes: whereas를 통해 외교적 전환에 성공한 사례와 실패한 사례를 한 문장 안에 대비시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: The case against a deep Israeli incursion
hard-won experience
decisive military victories were converted into diplomatic agreements
koreanAlignment.ko: 결정적 군사 승리를 외교 협정으로 전환한

koreanAlignment.en: the failure to anchor the 1967 victory in a political settlement
koreanAlignment.ko: 1967년 승리를 정치적 합의에 고정시키는 데 실패한 것

sentences.original: Israel's 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon, which most Israelis remember not for its tactical successes but for the blood and mud that consumed a generation of soldiers and their families, demonstrates what happens when military presence becomes an end in itself rather than a lever for negotiated outcomes -- a lesson that the current government appears unwilling to absorb even as it sells the public the dangerous illusion that firepower alone can dismantle Hizbullah and guarantee security for the north.
sentences.translation: 대부분의 이스라엘 국민이 전술적 성공이 아니라 한 세대의 군인과 그 가족을 집어삼킨 피와 진흙으로 기억하는 이스라엘의 남부 레바논 18년 점령은, 군사적 주둔이 협상 결과를 위한 지렛대가 아니라 그 자체가 목적이 될 때 무슨 일이 일어나는지를 보여준다 -- 현 정부가 화력만으로 히즈불라를 해체하고 북부 안보를 보장할 수 있다는 위험한 환상을 국민에게 파는 와중에도 받아들이려 하지 않는 교훈이다.

grammarElements.text: Israel's 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which most Israelis remember not for ... but for ...
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: demonstrates
grammarElements.label: 핵심 동사

grammarElements.text: what happens when military presence becomes an end in itself rather than a lever for negotiated outcomes
grammarElements.label: 명사절 목적어

vocabulary.example: Critics argue that sanctions alone cannot dismantle the regime.

expressions.usage: 수단이어야 할 것이 목적으로 변질될 때 비판적으로 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event] demonstrates what happens when [means] becomes an end in itself rather than [intended purpose]
patterns.explanation: 수단과 목적이 뒤바뀌었을 때의 결과를 역사적 사례로 보여주는 패턴
examples: The bailout demonstrates what happens when spending becomes an end in itself rather than a bridge to recovery.
The merger demonstrates what happens when growth becomes an end in itself rather than a path to profitability.

drillQuestions.question: Israel's 18-year occupation demonstrates what happens when military presence becomes an end in itself rather than a ______ for negotiated outcomes.

sentences.notes: not A but B 구조와 rather than 대비를 겹쳐 써서 점령의 진짜 유산이 무엇인지 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Israel's 18-year occupation of southern Lebanon
the blood and mud that consumed a generation
an end in itself rather than a lever for negotiated outcomes
koreanAlignment.ko: 협상 결과를 위한 지렛대가 아닌 그 자체가 목적

koreanAlignment.en: the dangerous illusion that firepower alone can dismantle Hizbullah
koreanAlignment.ko: 화력만으로 히즈불라를 해체할 수 있다는 위험한 환상

pronunciationNotes: dismantle은 '디스맨틀'로 두 번째 음절 강세, t는 묵음에 가까움

sentences.original: The paradox at the heart of the Iran war is that, while American and Israeli strikes have weakened the IRGC to a degree few thought possible -- sinking more than 100 ships, destroying missile depots and drone factories, killing over 3,000 Iranians -- the organisation's resilience is sustained by a cellular structure of up to 30,000 independent units designed to operate even if the governmental chain of command collapses, a contingency plan that makes the fantasy of neat regime change through air power alone precisely that: a fantasy.
sentences.translation: 이란 전쟁의 핵심에 있는 역설은, 미국과 이스라엘의 공습이 IRGC를 아무도 가능하리라 생각하지 못한 수준으로 약화시켰음에도 -- 100척 이상의 함선 격침, 미사일 저장고와 드론 공장 파괴, 3,000명 이상의 이란인 사망 -- 조직의 회복력이 정부 지휘 체계가 붕괴해도 작동하도록 설계된 최대 30,000개 독립 조직의 세포 구조에 의해 유지된다는 점이며, 이는 공중 화력만으로 깔끔하게 정권을 교체한다는 환상을 정확히 그것, 즉 환상으로 만드는 비상 계획이다.

grammarElements.text: The paradox at the heart of the Iran war
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: be동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: while American and Israeli strikes have weakened the IRGC
grammarElements.label: 양보절

grammarElements.text: the organisation's resilience is sustained by a cellular structure
grammarElements.label: 주절 (수동태)

vocabulary.example: The organisation's resilience surprised even seasoned intelligence analysts.
Every military plan needs a contingency for unexpected setbacks.

expressions.usage: 앞서 언급된 개념을 콜론으로 되풀이하며 수사적으로 강조하는 기법

patterns.pattern: The paradox is that, while [impressive result], [underlying reality contradicts it]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 성과와 그 이면의 모순을 대비시키는 패턴
examples: The paradox is that, while profits have soared, employee morale has collapsed.
The paradox is that, while the technology improves, its accessibility worsens.

drillQuestions.question: The paradox at the heart of the Iran war is that, while strikes have weakened the IRGC, the organisation's ______ is sustained by a cellular structure.

sentences.notes: while절의 양보와 콜론 뒤 반복('precisely that: a fantasy')이 역설을 극대화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The paradox at the heart of the Iran war
the organisation's resilience is sustained by a cellular structure
koreanAlignment.ko: 조직의 회복력이 세포 구조에 의해 유지된다

koreanAlignment.en: the fantasy of neat regime change through air power alone
koreanAlignment.ko: 공중 화력만으로 깔끔하게 정권을 교체한다는 환상

koreanAlignment.en: precisely that: a fantasy

sentences.original: Yet the IRGC is far more divided than its outward cohesion suggests, for an internal intelligence assessment reportedly found that half of its rank and file view membership as merely a job they would abandon for better employment, while much of the ideologically committed remainder is alienated by the corruption of senior commanders, misguided leadership policies, and the deeply unpopular order to turn weapons against Iran's own citizens during the protest waves that have periodically convulsed the country.
sentences.translation: 그러나 IRGC는 외적 결속력이 보여주는 것보다 훨씬 더 분열되어 있다. 내부 정보 평가에 따르면 일반 병사의 절반이 소속을 단지 더 나은 일자리가 생기면 버릴 직업 정도로 보고 있으며, 이념적으로 헌신하는 나머지의 상당수도 고위 지휘관의 부패와 잘못된 지도부 정책, 그리고 나라를 주기적으로 뒤흔든 시위 물결 중 이란 자국민에게 무기를 돌리라는 극히 인기 없는 명령에 의해 소외감을 느끼고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the IRGC
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is far more divided than
grammarElements.label: 비교급 동사구

grammarElements.text: its outward cohesion suggests
grammarElements.label: 비교 기준절

grammarElements.text: for an internal intelligence assessment reportedly found that ...
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (for)

grammarElements.text: while much of the ideologically committed remainder is alienated by ...
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (while)

vocabulary.example: The party's outward cohesion masked deep internal rifts.

expressions.usage: 조직의 지도부가 아닌 일반 대원이나 직원을 지칭할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Entity] is far more [adjective] than [outward appearance] suggests
patterns.explanation: 겉모습과 내부 실상의 괴리를 드러내는 패턴
examples: The alliance is far more fragile than its public unity suggests.
The economy is far more vulnerable than headline figures suggest.

drillQuestions.question: The IRGC is far more divided than its outward ______ suggests.

sentences.notes: far more ... than 비교급이 겉과 속의 괴리를 드러내고, for절과 while절이 분열의 두 층위를 나눈다.

koreanAlignment.en: far more divided than its outward cohesion suggests
koreanAlignment.ko: 외적 결속력이 보여주는 것보다 훨씬 더 분열된

koreanAlignment.en: rank and file
the deeply unpopular order to turn weapons against Iran's own citizens
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란 자국민에게 무기를 돌리라는 극히 인기 없는 명령

sentences.original: For Lebanon, the practical implications of Iran's fracturing are immense: Hizbullah, which has long depended on the IRGC's Quds Force for arms, training, and financial support channelled through opaque networks such as the Al-Qard al-Hassan lending institution, now faces the prospect that its Iranian lifeline will be severed or diminished -- a vulnerability that, combined with the military devastation Israel has already inflicted, creates what may be the most favourable conditions in a generation for the Lebanese state to reclaim authority over its own territory.
sentences.translation: 레바논에게 이란의 분열이 갖는 현실적 함의는 막대하다. 오랫동안 무기와 훈련, 알카르드 알하산 대출 기관 같은 불투명한 네트워크를 통해 전달되는 재정 지원을 IRGC 쿠드스 부대에 의존해 온 히즈불라는, 이제 이란의 생명줄이 끊기거나 줄어들 전망에 직면해 있으며 -- 이스라엘이 이미 가한 군사적 파괴와 결합된 이 취약성은, 레바논 국가가 자국 영토에 대한 권위를 되찾기 위해 한 세대 만에 가장 유리한 조건을 만들어 낸다.

grammarElements.text: For Lebanon
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (관점 한정)

grammarElements.text: the practical implications of Iran's fracturing
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are immense
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: Hizbullah ... now faces the prospect that its Iranian lifeline will be severed or diminished
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 부연절

vocabulary.example: The fund's opaque structure made it impossible to trace the money.
The sanctions threatened to sever the country's last financial lifeline.

expressions.phrase: the most favourable conditions in a generation
expressions.meaning: 한 세대 만에 가장 유리한 조건
expressions.usage: 기회의 희소성과 역사적 중대함을 동시에 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: For [affected party], the implications of [event] are immense: [specific consequence]
patterns.explanation: 특정 주체에게 미치는 영향을 콜론 뒤에 구체적으로 풀어내는 패턴
examples: For Europe, the implications of the trade war are immense: supply chains will need complete restructuring.
For small banks, the implications of tighter regulation are immense: compliance costs could double.

drillQuestions.question: For Lebanon, the practical implications of Iran's fracturing are ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤에 히즈불라의 구체적 취약성을 전개하며 레바논에게 열린 기회의 창을 논증한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the practical implications of Iran's fracturing
opaque networks
its Iranian lifeline will be severed or diminished
the most favourable conditions in a generation
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 세대 만에 가장 유리한 조건

sentences.original: The five-step plan advanced by the Lebanese parliamentarian Fouad Makhzoumi -- declaring Beirut a weapons-free city, deploying the army across every district including those long treated as untouchable, establishing permanent checkpoints, granting the armed forces full authority to seize illegal weapons, and implementing a strategic doctrine for civilian protection -- is conceptually straightforward, yet its implementation would require a degree of political courage that Lebanon's consensus-paralysed elites have consistently failed to muster, preferring the deferral of difficult decisions to the risk of confronting Hizbullah's entrenched dual identity as both political party and armed militia.
sentences.translation: 레바논 국회의원 푸아드 마흐주미가 제시한 5단계 계획 -- 베이루트를 비무장 도시로 선언하고, 오랫동안 손댈 수 없다 취급받던 지역을 포함해 모든 구역에 군대를 배치하며, 영구 검문소를 설치하고, 불법 무기 압수에 대한 완전한 권한을 군에 부여하고, 민간인 보호를 위한 전략 교리를 시행하는 것 -- 은 개념적으로 명쾌하지만, 그 실행에는 합의 마비에 빠진 레바논 엘리트층이 일관되게 발휘하지 못한 수준의 정치적 용기가 필요하며, 그들은 히즈불라의 정당이자 무장 민병대라는 뿌리 깊은 이중 정체성에 맞서는 위험보다 어려운 결정의 유예를 선호해 왔다.

grammarElements.text: The five-step plan advanced by the Lebanese parliamentarian
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: -- declaring ... and implementing --
grammarElements.label: 대시 사이 삽입 나열

grammarElements.text: is conceptually straightforward
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: yet its implementation would require a degree of political courage that ...
grammarElements.label: 양보·대조 접속

vocabulary.example: Entrenched interests blocked every attempt at reform.

expressions.usage: 의사결정 구조가 만장일치 요구로 움직이지 못하는 상황을 비판할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Plan/Proposal] is conceptually straightforward, yet its implementation would require [difficult condition]
patterns.explanation: 이론과 실행 사이의 간극을 yet으로 대비시키는 패턴
examples: The reform is conceptually straightforward, yet its implementation would require bipartisan cooperation that seems impossible.
The solution is conceptually straightforward, yet its implementation would require a regulatory overhaul.

drillQuestions.question: The five-step plan is conceptually straightforward, yet its ______ would require a degree of political courage.

sentences.notes: 대시 안에 5단계를 나열한 뒤 yet으로 이론과 실행의 간극을 드러내는 고급 문장 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The five-step plan advanced by the Lebanese parliamentarian
is conceptually straightforward
consensus-paralysed elites
Hizbullah's entrenched dual identity as both political party and armed militia
koreanAlignment.ko: 히즈불라의 정당이자 무장 민병대라는 뿌리 깊은 이중 정체성

sentences.original: The most promising path to a stable post-war order in both Iran and Lebanon may run through the kind of broad-based political transition envisioned by Mohsen Sazegara, one of the IRGC's own founders, who argues that a National Reconciliation Council incorporating republicans, monarchists, ethnic groups, and even supporters of the current system could redirect the loyalty of disillusioned guardsmen away from a discredited theocracy and toward the defence of Iranian territory and its people -- though such an outcome would require America to make the council's formation an explicit condition for ending the war, rather than pursuing the more seductive but ultimately hollow goal of picking Iran's next leader as it did in Venezuela.
sentences.translation: 이란과 레바논 모두에서 안정적 전후 질서로 가는 가장 유망한 길은 IRGC 창설자 중 한 명인 모흐센 사제가라가 구상한 광범위한 정치적 전환을 거칠 수 있다. 그는 공화주의자, 군주주의자, 소수 민족, 심지어 현 체제 지지자까지 포함하는 국민화해위원회가 환멸을 느낀 혁명수비대원의 충성을 신뢰를 잃은 신정정치로부터 이란 영토와 국민 방어 쪽으로 돌릴 수 있다고 주장한다 -- 다만 이런 결과는 미국이 베네수엘라에서 그랬듯 이란의 다음 지도자를 고르는 유혹적이지만 궁극적으로 공허한 목표를 추구하는 대신, 위원회 구성을 전쟁 종결의 명시적 조건으로 삼아야 가능하다.

grammarElements.text: The most promising path to a stable post-war order
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: may run through
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: the kind of broad-based political transition envisioned by Mohsen Sazegara
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: though such an outcome would require America to make ...
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (though)

vocabulary.example: The revolution transformed a monarchy into a theocracy.

expressions.usage: 겉보기엔 매력적이지만 실질적 성과 없는 정책을 비판할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The most promising path to [goal] may run through [unexpected means]
patterns.explanation: 기대 밖의 경로가 최선의 해법일 수 있음을 제시하는 패턴
examples: The most promising path to peace may run through economic integration.
The most promising path to reform may run through grassroots mobilisation rather than legislation.

drillQuestions.question: The most promising path to a stable post-war order may run ______ the kind of broad-based political transition.

sentences.notes: though절이 희망적 전망에 현실적 조건을 붙이며, rather than으로 미국이 피해야 할 전철을 경고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The most promising path to a stable post-war order
koreanAlignment.ko: 안정적 전후 질서로 가는 가장 유망한 길

koreanAlignment.en: broad-based political transition
a discredited theocracy
the more seductive but ultimately hollow goal

sentences.original: What unites the crises in Lebanon and Iran is the stubborn lesson that external military force, however overwhelming, cannot substitute for internal political legitimacy: Israel cannot bomb Hizbullah out of Lebanese politics any more than America can bomb the Islamic Republic into a stable democracy, and the attempt to do so risks deepening the very instability it aims to resolve -- producing not order but competing centres of armed power in Tehran and a Beirut that remains, as it has for decades, a capital governed by ambiguity.
sentences.translation: 레바논과 이란의 위기를 하나로 묶는 것은, 외부 군사력이 아무리 압도적이더라도 내부의 정치적 정당성을 대체할 수 없다는 완고한 교훈이다. 이스라엘이 폭격으로 히즈불라를 레바논 정치에서 몰아낼 수 없는 것은, 미국이 폭격으로 이슬람 공화국을 안정된 민주주의로 만들 수 없는 것과 마찬가지이며, 그렇게 하려는 시도는 해결하고자 하는 바로 그 불안정을 심화시킬 위험이 있다 -- 질서가 아니라 테헤란의 경쟁하는 무장 세력 중심지들과, 수십 년째 모호함에 의해 통치되는 수도 베이루트를 낳으면서.

grammarElements.text: What unites the crises in Lebanon and Iran
grammarElements.label: 명사절 주어 (what절)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: the stubborn lesson that external military force cannot substitute for internal political legitimacy
grammarElements.label: 보어 + that절

grammarElements.text: Israel cannot bomb Hizbullah out of Lebanese politics any more than America can bomb ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 부연 (no more ... than 비교)

vocabulary.example: No government can survive long without political legitimacy.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 불가능한 일을 동치시켜 논점을 강화하는 수사적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actor A] cannot [action] any more than [Actor B] can [parallel action]
patterns.explanation: 두 개의 불가능을 동치시켜 핵심 주장을 강화하는 패턴
examples: A company cannot buy loyalty any more than a government can legislate trust.
Technology cannot replace judgment any more than data can replace wisdom.

drillQuestions.question: Israel cannot bomb Hizbullah out of Lebanese politics any more than America can bomb the Islamic Republic into a stable ______.

sentences.notes: What절 주어와 no more ... than 구조가 결합해 기사 전체의 핵심 명제를 한 문장으로 집약한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What unites the crises in Lebanon and Iran
koreanAlignment.ko: 레바논과 이란의 위기를 하나로 묶는 것

koreanAlignment.en: external military force cannot substitute for internal political legitimacy
koreanAlignment.ko: 외부 군사력이 내부의 정치적 정당성을 대체할 수 없다

koreanAlignment.en: a capital governed by ambiguity

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: parallelism (cannot A any more than B)

sentences.original: The window for converting military gains into durable political settlements -- in Lebanon through the structured assertion of state sovereignty over Hizbullah's parallel army, and in Iran through a negotiated transition that offers the IRGC's rank and file a future worth choosing over martyrdom -- will not remain open indefinitely, and if it closes because strategists in Jerusalem, Washington, and the Gulf mistook the destruction of buildings for the transformation of societies, the region will be left not with peace but with the certainty of another round of violence, and then another, in the mud and rubble that always outlast the bombs.
sentences.translation: 군사적 성과를 지속 가능한 정치적 합의로 전환하는 기회의 창 -- 레바논에서는 히즈불라의 평행 군대에 대한 국가 주권의 체계적 주장을 통해, 이란에서는 IRGC 일반 대원에게 순교보다 나은 선택지가 되는 미래를 제시하는 협상된 전환을 통해 -- 은 무기한 열려 있지 않을 것이며, 만약 예루살렘과 워싱턴, 걸프의 전략가들이 건물의 파괴를 사회의 변혁으로 착각했기 때문에 그 창이 닫힌다면, 이 지역에 남는 것은 평화가 아니라 또 한 차례의 폭력, 그리고 또 한 차례의 폭력이 확실히 반복되리라는 것이며, 그것은 폭탄보다 항상 오래 남는 진흙과 잔해 속에서 일어날 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The window for converting military gains into durable political settlements
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: will not remain open indefinitely
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: -- in Lebanon through ... and in Iran through --
grammarElements.label: 대시 사이 병렬 삽입구

grammarElements.text: if it closes because strategists ... mistook the destruction of buildings for the transformation of societies
grammarElements.label: 조건 + 이유 복합절

vocabulary.example: A durable settlement requires buy-in from all major factions.
The leadership glorified martyrdom to maintain the ranks' morale.

expressions.phrase: mistook the destruction of buildings for the transformation of societies
expressions.usage: 물리적 성과와 근본적 변화를 혼동하는 오류를 날카롭게 지적하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The window for [opportunity] will not remain open indefinitely, and if it closes because [mistake], [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 기회의 시한성과 그 기회를 놓쳤을 때의 결과를 경고하는 패턴
examples: The window for diplomatic resolution will not remain open indefinitely, and if it closes because leaders chose posturing over negotiation, the cost will be borne by civilians.
The window for reform will not remain open indefinitely, and if it closes because of political cowardice, the next crisis will be worse.

drillQuestions.question: The window for converting military gains into durable political settlements will not remain open ______.

sentences.notes: 기사의 결론부로, 대시 삽입구와 if-because 복합절을 통해 기회의 시한성과 실패의 대가를 경고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The window for converting military gains into durable political settlements
koreanAlignment.ko: 군사적 성과를 지속 가능한 정치적 합의로 전환하는 기회의 창

koreanAlignment.en: will not remain open indefinitely
mistook the destruction of buildings for the transformation of societies
the mud and rubble that always outlast the bombs
koreanAlignment.ko: 폭탄보다 항상 오래 남는 진흙과 잔해
